embed.alt: A cinematic surreal scene in a dim modern apartment at night, rain on the window, neon glow through blinds. A man stares at his hands, breathing hard. A woman steps in, shaken. Quick dream glitches: clock ticking backwards, lights pulsing, thunder repeating. The man whispers: “This isn’t real.” The woman answers, melodramatic: “It’s a dream… inside another dream.” Fast push-in closeups as the room subtly warps. He panics: “Then how do we wake up?” She grabs his arm: “What if waking up sends us deeper?” Rapid jump-cut montage of their faces repeating like fractured timelines. He asks: “Can we escape?” She stares past him: “Only if we remember who’s dreaming.”
